question_answered: Does no activation checkpointing make memory much higher, and does sparse query-recompute no-AC raise memory further?
short_answer: No activation checkpointing is memory-expensive. In the aligned 4xMI350 FSDP4/EP4 S4096 run, sparse query-recompute no-AC peaked at 146.99 GiB/GPU. Query-recompute can remove selected sparse-attention saved-state slabs, so it is not automatically higher than a comparable no-AC baseline, but this EP4 target slice is materially higher memory than the earlier 8x FSDP8/EP8 no-AC evidence.

run.external_workload_note: GPUs 0-3 were occupied by a separate Qwen FT torchrun job, so this aligned EP4 profile used the free 4-GPU slice.

comparison.not_apples_to_apples_warning: The earlier no-AC expandable baseline was 8x FSDP8/EP8 GBS128; this run is the corrected aligned 4x FSDP4/EP4 GBS64 slice because only GPUs 4-7 were free.
comparison.read: Query-recompute gives a modest materialized throughput lift in this aligned EP4 profile, but with high no-AC memory. A same-shape EP4 no-AC non-query-recompute control is needed to prove whether query-recompute itself increases or decreases peak memory at EP4.

profile_summary.read: The aligned EP4 profile still points at two active tracks: sparse shared-KV query-recompute backward is visible, and MORI routed owner-rank VJP plus dX return skew remains a major multi-GPU bottleneck. The no-AC memory cost is real; it should be compared against a same-shape EP4 control before promotion.

commands.launch_summary: docker torchrun via launchers/torchtitan_direct_deepseek_v4_4x8xmi350_canary.sh with CANARY_VISIBLE_DEVICES=4,5,6,7 CANARY_EP=4 CANARY_GBS=64 CANARY_STEPS=6 CANARY_CHECKPOINT_ENABLE=false CANARY_ACTIVATION_CHECKPOINT_MODE=none CANARY_SHAREDKV_BACKWARD_MODE=query_recompute CANARY_DSV4_PROFILE_SHAREDKV_BACKWARD=true CANARY_MORI_AITER_TIMING_PATH and CANARY_DSV4_PROFILE_TIMING_PATH under /scratch

next_actions: Run a same-shape 4x FSDP4/EP4 no-AC control without sparse query-recompute if the code path still exists, to isolate query-recompute memory delta.
Run the preferred 8x target with EP4 once GPUs 0-3 are free, because the user requested EP4 even on 8 GPUs.
Continue sparse track by reducing shared-KV query-recompute backward boundary and avoiding selected-state/prob/grad-logit materialization.
Continue MoE track by reducing owner-rank VJP tail and dX return skew rather than only tuning MORI combine.
